conversation.message: How have you been feeling lately, overall?
I don't know... just tired, I suppose. I have a lot on my plate, but physically I feel drained. It's hard to explain.
Can you tell me more about what feeling drained is like for you?
It's like I'm running out of steam. I try to do things, but my body just feels heavy. Even small things, like walking up the stairs, feel like climbing a mountain.
Is it harder to focus or concentrate than it used to be?
Yes, definitely. I'll be reading something and suddenly I'll just zone out. I look up and I've read the same sentence three times without noticing. It's like my brain isn't really working anymore.
How long has it been since you first noticed yourself reading the same sentence over and over?
A few months, maybe? It started slowly, but now it's just normal. I don't know if I'm just getting older or what.
How have you been sleeping lately?
Not great. I wake up a lot, usually around two or three. I can't relax, so I just lie there and think about things.
How long do you usually lie awake thinking before you fall back asleep?
Hours, probably. I get stuck in my head and can't turn off the thoughts.
Been doing anything you enjoy lately?
Not really. I used to love gardening, but now it just feels like too much effort. I don't know if I'm just losing my energy or what.
How long has it felt like too much effort to garden?
A few weeks, I suppose. It used to be my favorite thing to do, but now I just look at the plants and think, 'I don't have the energy for this.'
When you think about the next few weeks, what do you expect day to day?
More of the same, I suppose. Just trying to get through each day without collapsing.
What does "collapsing" feel like for you?
Just... exhaustion. Physically and mentally. I don't know if I'm just tired or if something is actually wrong, but it feels like I'm running out of time.
Do you find it harder to make decisions than before, even small ones?
Yes, definitely. I'll be standing in the kitchen and I'll look at two things and just freeze because I can't decide which one to do. It's silly, I know, but...
How long do you usually find yourself frozen like that?
Long enough to wonder if I'm going crazy, probably.
Do you feel like you have enough energy for your usual day?
No, not really. It feels like I'm running on empty most of the time.
What does "running on empty" feel like for you?
Like there's nothing left to give.
Are you still into the things you used to care about?
Not really. I used to love reading and watching movies, but now... I just don't have the energy for it.
How long has it felt like too much effort to enjoy a book or a film?
A few months, I suppose. It used to be my go-to way to relax, but now it just feels like too much work.
Is there anything weighing on your mind that you wish you could change?
Just... everything, I suppose. I feel like I'm stuck in this rut and I don't know how to get out of it.
When you say "everything," what's one part that feels heaviest right now?
Just the idea that I'm not getting any younger and I'm not sure if things will ever get better.
How do you feel about how things have gone for you recently?
Like I've lost my way, I suppose.
What part of losing your way feels most difficult right now?
Just the fact that I don't know how to find it again.
How's your appetite been?
I'm eating, but I don't really taste anything.
Has this change in taste made you eat less than usual?
Yes, probably. I'm not really hungry enough to make a fuss about it.
Have you found yourself getting emotional more easily lately?
Yes... I get teary just thinking about the future.
What about the future makes you feel that way?
Just the idea of... more of this, I suppose.
When did this sense of emptiness start feeling this strong?
I don't know... a few months ago, I think.
Is it more 'heavy and slow' or 'drained after little effort'?
More heavy and slow, I suppose.
Can you tell me more about what that heaviness feels like?
Like a weight on my chest.
Does rest actually help, or are you still flat afterward?
I sleep, but I'm still tired when I wake up.
How long has that tiredness lasted after waking up?
Hours, usually.
Even after sleep, do you wake up already tired?
Yes, I do.
How long has this been happening?
A few months, I suppose.
Does fatigue hit you in waves or stay steady all day?
Steady, mostly.
Can you describe what that steady fatigue feels like?
Like a constant fog.
When you say things have been rough, is that most days or just now and then?
Most days, probably.
Can you tell me a bit more about what makes those days feel that way?
Just... everything.
Does anything briefly lift your mood, or does it stay flat?
It usually goes right back down.
When it goes back down, what's that like for you?
Just... more tired.
When you try to do something you used to like, what happens in the moment?
I usually just stop.
What makes you decide to stop?
I don't know. I just... can't.
Is it more 'not fun' or 'can't be bothered to start'?
Can't be bothered.
